feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Africa CDC confirms Marburg case in Uganda as Ebola outbreak rages
entry.summary: Early reports indicate there may be another case, but spread is thought to be localized.

entry.title: He thought it was just a rash. He nearly died of AIDS.
entry.summary: He had no idea he was infected with HIV. It progressed to AIDS, and he nearly died before learning the truth.

entry.title: Woman’s hip replacement disintegrates, causing severe metal poisoning
entry.summary: Doctors find grey fluid and dead, metallic flesh inside poisoned woman's hip.

entry.title: What is cyclosporiasis? Know the signs, prevention as Michigan health officials investigate outbreak
entry.summary: Michigan health officials are investigating an outbreak of cyclosporiasis across the state after at least 170 cases were reported within days.

entry.title: Alcohol is one of the most dangerous drugs, yet its presence is ubiquitous in social settings and celebrations
entry.summary: Despite growing evidence of alcohol’s harms, it remains deeply embedded in social norms and cultural rituals, both in the US and abroad.

entry.title: Cheap Daily Supplements May Boost Brain Functions in Older People
entry.summary: Cheap daily supplements are changing how scientists view the gut and its connection to the brain.

entry.title: Beachgoers warned of flesh-eating bacteria ahead of July 4
entry.summary: The bacteria can enter the body through open wounds or by consuming raw oysters.

entry.title: 5 of America's greatest medical breakthroughs revealed as the nation marks 250 years
entry.summary: From joint replacement and Deep TMS for depression to targeted therapy for cancer, experts highlight top U.S. medical breakthroughs over 250 years.

entry.title: Another Thing for Millennial Women to Stress Over
entry.summary: Shingles cases are rising sharply among 30-to-50-year-olds, especially women, Claire Trageser writes for The Cut. Our stressful lives are to blame.

entry.title: Sorry, But These Healthy Foods Contain Way Less Fiber Than You Think
entry.summary: This may make you rethink your fibermaxxing plan.

entry.title: 4 surprising (and evidence-based) health benefits of drinking coffee
entry.summary: Hundreds of studies have shown that a cup of joe — or more! — every day may protect against some major diseases.

entry.title: ‘Super movers’ have half the risk of brain decline in old age — even if their brains show signs of dementia
entry.summary: Research found that a group of older adults may maintain stronger cognitive despite signs of brain aging. The findings highlight the link between mobility, lifestyle and cognitive resilience.

entry.title: Experimental Drug Stops 90% of Pancreatic Cancer Cell Spread in Lab Tests
entry.summary: Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, the most common form of pancreatic cancer, is particularly deadly and hard to treat.

entry.title: On the front line of Congo’s Ebola outbreak
entry.summary: Al Jazeera’s Catherine Wambua-Soi reports from Bunia, on the front line of the Ebola response.

entry.title: These longevity exercises are going viral—and why a physical therapist recommends them for stability and fall prevention
entry.summary: A yoga teacher’s anti-aging hack has been watched millions of times online

entry.title: US life expectancy on track to reach record high as death rate falls to record low in 2025
entry.summary: New mortality data from the federal government suggests that life expectancy probably hit another record high in 2025, as death rates have continued to fall since the height of the Covid-19 pandemic.

entry.title: Canadian boy, 11, dies of rabies after waking to bat on his face
entry.summary: Rabies infections are rare in Canada. There have been 28 human deaths due to rabies in the country since 1924.

entry.title: West Nile virus is surging earlier than it has in decades. Here’s how to protect yourself.
entry.summary: The spread of the mosquito-borne virus is off to its earliest start in more than 20 years.

entry.title: Single Injection Reverses Osteoarthritis in Animals in Just 4 Weeks
entry.summary: The chronic loss of joint cartilage known as osteoarthritis causes pain and bone decay for hundreds of millions of people every day.

entry.title: Identification of cross-stage, cross-species malaria CD8+ T cell antigens
entry.summary: An immunopeptidomics analysis identifies numerous Plasmodium T cell antigens that are conserved across malaria parasite species and expressed at several phases of the parasite’s life cycle, with possible applications in the development of a malaria vaccine.

entry.title: West Nile virus detected in mosquitoes from these 8 Orange County cities
entry.summary: Orange County vector control officials announced Wednesday that West Nile virus was confirmed this week in samples collected from several cities, including Costa Mesa, Fullerton, Garden Grove and Westminster. Last week, officials reported samples infected wit…

entry.title: Steatosis shapes prognosis-defining liver metastasis heterogeneity in CRC
entry.summary: Liver steatosis increases formation of replacement liver metastases by increasing MYC stability through acetylation, which increases proline synthesis and collagen production.

entry.title: Restoring cortical disinhibition improves Huntington’s disease phenotypes
entry.summary: In a mouse model of Huntington’s disease, targeted modulation of neuronal activity restores network function and improves motor deficits.

entry.title: Dual tumour–myeloid targeting of glioblastoma with GPNMB CAR-T cells
entry.summary: Integrated multi-omic profiling of glioblastoma reveals GPNMB as a shared antigen in tumour cells and the surrounding microenvironment, and GPNMB-targeted chimeric antigen receptor T cells demonstrate therapeutic activity in vitro and in animal models.

entry.title: Tattoos Affect Your Immune System in Ways Scientists Are Just Beginning to Understand
entry.summary: Humans have been tattooing themselves for thousands of years, for spiritual, cultural, or personal reasons.

entry.title: Your guide to identifying some of the most common ticks found in the US
entry.summary: As awareness of tickborne diseases increases, it's important to know which ticks can cause these diseases, where you can find them and how to identify them.

entry.title: Company Plans to Drop 600,000 Bacteria-Infected Mosquitoes into DC This Summer
entry.summary: Wolbachia bacteria could offer an environmentally-friendly way of controlling mosquitoes and the many diseases they spread.

entry.title: West Nile virus detected in southern state as health officials warn residents about mosquitoes
entry.summary: Nashville detects West Nile virus in mosquitoes for the first time in 2026, prompting officials to urge residents to eliminate standing water and use DEET.

entry.title: The link between hot weather and kidney stones
entry.summary: Higher temperatures are linked to a surge in kidney stones diagnoses. Here's why and how global warming is expected to increase cases.

entry.title: Are frozen and canned vegetables as good as the fresh ones? Here's what you need to know
entry.summary: You probably know fruit and vegetables are good for you, and most of us don’t eat enough of them. But amid a cost-of-living crisis, buying more fresh food

entry.title: New Review Finds Creatine Shows Promise for Depression With Major Caveats
entry.summary: The evidence is mixed, but brain-energy research has opened a serious question.

entry.title: Heat wave warning: Common medications that can increase your risk of heat illness
entry.summary: A heat wave can be dangerous for anyone, but some everyday prescription and over-the-counter medications can make it harder for the body to handle high heat, increasing the risk of dehydration, dizziness, fainting, and heat illness.

entry.title: CDC seeks source of parasitic outbreak linked to ‘explosive’ diarrhea in multiple states
entry.summary: People usually become infected from eating contaminated food or water.

entry.title: Genome-wide association studies of infant and toddler temperament in European and multi-ancestry populations
entry.summary: A genome-wide association study of infant and toddler temperament in up to 78,000 infants found new genetic loci. Infant and toddler temperament showed significant single-nucleotide polymorphism heritability and genetic overlap with later life outcomes.

entry.title: 11-year-old boy dies from rabies after waking up to bat on his face
entry.summary: A new medical report details how an 11-year-old boy died of rabies after waking up with a bat on his face while sleeping. Here's more on his death.

entry.title: ‘World’s most contagious disease’ on track for highest US cases in 35 years — doc warns: ‘We are allowing it to spread’
entry.summary: “This is a major public health warning sign,” epidemiologist Dr. Syra Madad told The Post. “The US is already close to last year’s total with about half the year still ahead.&#822…

entry.title: A Philly woman knew something was wrong. It took 4 ER visits to find her cancer
entry.summary: Medical advances mean that more people are surviving cancer. But not everyone is benefiting from those gains equally, a new report shows.

entry.title: My Boyfriend and I Signed Up for a Summer of Madness. Now Our Sex Life Is Reaping the Consequences.
entry.summary: Why did we do this?

entry.title: Tips to prevent cyclospora infection and explosive diarrhea
entry.summary: At least 170 Michiganders have contracted a parasitic gastrointestinal infection called cyclosporiasis. Here are the CDC's tips to prevent illness.

entry.title: 6-year-old girl bitten by rabid bat while playing outside her Wisconsin home, family says
entry.summary: The girl’s mother said her sons killed the bat with a homemade “Braveheart”-inspired sword. Her daughter is recovering.

entry.title: Even high amounts of coffee may lower your risk for liver disease
entry.summary: Drinking coffee may lower your risk for liver cancer and other liver disease, according to a new study.

entry.title: The Best Vegetable to Help You Poop, According to Dietitians
entry.summary: Do peas help with constipation? Green peas provide fiber, fluids and magnesium to support regularity and digestive health. Learn how peas help you poop.

entry.title: After 14 Months of Chemotherapy, Girl, 5, Has One Request: 'Can We Go to the Beach?'
entry.summary: Maddie Fries underwent a brutal treatment schedule to preserve her vision after doctors discovered tumors on her optic nerves

entry.title: Canadian boy, 11, died from rabies after waking up with bat on his mouth
entry.summary: An 11-year-old Canadian boy has died from rabies after waking up with a bat on his nose and mouth.

entry.title: The Best Times to Drink Coffee for Energy, According to Experts
entry.summary: Many people rely on coffee to feel alert and energetic, but drinking your cup of joe at these specific times can maximize its energy-boosting benefits.

entry.title: Conan O’Brien reveals overlooked male menopause change that surprised him
entry.summary: The comedian and former late-night host opened up about the moment he realized one of the effects of aging may have been tied to his changing testosterone levels.

entry.title: America at 300: Imagining the next half-century of change
entry.summary: Moon bases, living to 100 and (finally!) flying cars. What 2076 and our future will look like may be stranger than we expect.

entry.title: Column | There’s a new way to treat sprains and strains. Hint: Ice is out.
entry.summary: If you think you need rest, ice and ibuprofen, think again.

entry.title: 8 Fruits That Fight Inflammation and Support Gut Health
entry.summary: Experts break down how powerful antioxidants and fiber can make fruit a great choice for your gut health—and the one tip you need to know to prevent blood sugar spikes.

entry.title: Peptide hype is at an all-time high – I found out the truth
entry.summary: They’re in skincare, lip glosses and weight-loss drugs – but also in ‘grey-market’ injectables where some people are buying products online without sufficient human trials to prove their safety. Charlotte Cripps samples some for herself and learns more about …
